user.title: Add HomieProxy instance
user.description: Create a new HTTP proxy endpoint. An initial access token will be generated automatically — you can manage tokens later via the integration's Configure dialog.

data.name: Endpoint name
data.restrict_out_cidrs: Custom outbound CIDRs (one per line, or comma-separated — only used when 'Custom CIDR list' is selected)
data.restrict_in_cidrs: Inbound IP CIDR allow-list (one per line, or comma-separated — blank = allow any)
data.requires_auth: Require Home Assistant authentication on top of the token

error.invalid_name_chars: Name may only contain letters, digits, hyphen, underscore, and dot
error.custom_requires_cidrs: Custom mode requires at least one CIDR
error.already_configured: An endpoint with this name already exists

abort.already_configured: An endpoint with this name already exists

init.title: Configure HomieProxy
init.description: Pick a section to edit. Changes apply immediately when you save each step.

rename.title: Rename endpoint
rename.description:
Current name: **{current_name}**

The name is the last segment of the endpoint URL — i.e. the part after `/api/homie_proxy/`. Changing it immediately updates the endpoint URL, so update any clients that reference the old name.

tokens.title: Manage access tokens
tokens.description: Currently {count} token(s). Edit the list (one per line) or pick an action below. Choosing an action other than 'Save changes' will append/replace tokens — pick 'Save changes' afterward to persist the result.

restrictions.title: Network restrictions
restrictions.description: Outbound mode controls which target URLs the proxy will call. 'Custom CIDR list' uses the outbound CIDRs below; the other modes ignore *and clear* that field on save, so stale entries don't sit around. Inbound CIDRs limit which client IPs may reach this endpoint (blank = any).{bad_hint}

data.restrict_out_cidrs: Outbound CIDRs (one per line, or comma-separated)
data.restrict_in_cidrs: Inbound CIDRs (one per line, or comma-separated)

settings.title: Auth, timeout & streaming
settings.description: **Require HA auth** adds Home Assistant login on top of the proxy token — recommended when the endpoint is internet-facing. **Require HA auth for debug page** gates `/api/homie_proxy/debug`; disable only on trusted local networks. **Stream chunk size**: 0 = low-latency (recommended for live MJPEG/HLS — yields bytes as they arrive). Any value > 0 buffers up to N bytes per write, trading latency for throughput. For live video, 0 is almost always correct.

data.requires_auth: Require Home Assistant authentication on top of the token
data.debug_requires_auth: Require Home Assistant authentication for the debug page

info.title: Endpoint info (with help + examples)
